title: Bridge Essay: Psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression: fMRI-measured brain mechanisms.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.

**Psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression: fMRI-measured brain mechanisms.**. Sci Rep.. PMID: 29030624. DOI: 10.1038/s41598-017-13282-7. Psilocybin with psychological support is showing promise as atreatment model in psychiatry but its therapeutic mechanisms are poorly understood. Here, cerebral blood flow (CBF) and blood oxygen-level dependent (BOLD) resting-state functional connectivity (RSFC) were measured with functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) before and after treatment with psilocybin (serotonin agonist) for treatment-resistant depression (TRD).
